Article Title: Nominee vs legal heir: What is the difference and who has claim on assets? All you need to know
Article Text:
AI image

Who is a Nominee?



Who is a Legal Heir?

Difference between a Nominee and a Legal heir

Aspect Nominee Legal Heir Role Custodian Owner Transfer of Assets Can be done without any legal formalities. Requires testamentary documents for proof of ownership. Powers No power to transact, use, or transfer assets. Power to use and transact assets as an owner. Appointment Owner can appoint anyone, but some institutions specify certain conditions. Determined by succession/personal laws. Legal Right Cannot contest ownership legally. Can contest ownership in a court of law.

When individuals open bank accounts, invest in mutual funds, or buy insurance, they are often asked to name a nominee.However, many people mistakenly assume that the nominee automatically inherits the assets. In reality, the nominee is not always the final recipient — the ownership rights belong to the legal heir, reported ET.Understanding the distinction between these two is essential to ensure that your wealth is passed on to the intended beneficiaries.A nominee acts as a temporary custodian of the deceased person’s assets.The nominee’s role is limited to holding and managing the assets until they are legally transferred to the rightful heirs.According to ET, the nominee cannot sell, trade, or make any transactional decisions about the assets.The account or policy owner can appoint anyone — family, friends, or acquaintances — as a nominee, though financial institutions often prefer close family members.In some cases, the nominee and the legal heir can be the same person, which helps in ensuring a smoother transfer of assets.The legal heir or beneficiary is the rightful owner of the assets once the original holder passes away.To claim ownership, the legal heir must produce valid documents such as a will or other testamentary proof.If there is no will — a situation known as dying intestate — the heir is determined as per the country’s succession or personal laws, reported ET.While a nominee can initially access the assets, only the legal heir can claim them permanently or challenge ownership in court.Experts quoted by ET emphasise that having clarity on this difference helps avoid disputes and ensures that the savings reach the intended family members or beneficiaries.Therefore, it is advisable to regularly update both nominees and wills to align with one’s estate planning goals.
Article Summary: A nominee acts as a temporary custodian of the deceased person’s assets. The legal heir or beneficiary is the rightful owner of the assets once the original holder passes away. To claim ownership, the legal heir must produce valid documents such as a will.

Article Title: Life Certificate 2025: Step-by-step guide to generate and submit Jeevan Pramaan online and offline
Article Text:
What is a Life Certificate and why does it matter



Annual submission deadline for pensioners in 2025



1 November and 30 November.

How to generate Jeevan Pramaan online: Step-by-step process



Step 1: Biometric device registration

Step 2: Authentication and certificate generation

Electronic delivery and access of Digital Life Certificate



How can you submit a Life Certificate in 2025



Offline submission at a bank branch or CSC centre

Online submission via Jeevan Pramaan Portal

Submission through the UMANG App

Who needs to submit the Life Certificate



Central and State Government pensioners

Defence pensioners

Family pensioners in the event of the pensioner’s death

Pensioners from public sector undertakings

Key details required before submitting the Life Certificate



Aadhaar Number

Registered Mobile Number linked with Aadhaar

Pension Payment Order (PPO) Number

Bank Account Number where the pension is credited

Name of the Pension Sanctioning Authority

Is Aadhaar mandatory for a digital Life Certificate



Legal validity of Jeevan Pramaan



How Jeevan Pramaan simplifies pensioner verification



Every year, lakhs of pensioners across India must prove that they are still alive to continue receiving their pension. This verification, known as the Life Certificate or Digital Life Certificate (DLC), is a crucial and mandatory step in ensuring that pension payments are made only to eligible recipients, preventing fraud and misuse.Traditionally, this process involved visiting the bank or pension office in person, standing in long queues, and submitting a signed paper certificate. However, with the government’s focus on digitisation and technological advancement, this process has become much simpler, faster, and more convenient. Pensioners can now submit their certificates digitally from the comfort of their homes using Jeevan Pramaan, a secure, user-friendly, and Aadhaar-based platform.A Life Certificate is an official document that confirms a pensioner is alive. Without it, pension payments are automatically paused by the pension disbursing agency. This verification is essential for preventing fraudulent claims and ensuring that pensions reach the rightful beneficiaries.The Digital Life Certificate (DLC), also known as Jeevan Pramaan, is an advanced biometric-enabled version of the traditional paper certificate.Introduced by the Government of India, it uses Aadhaar-based biometric authentication using either fingerprint or iris scan to verify a pensioner’s identity. Once verified, a digital certificate is automatically generated and shared with authorised pension disbursing agencies.This online process eliminates the need for physical verification, providing elderly pensioners with a simpler and more accessible way to maintain their pension benefits.According to PIB, The submission of the Life Certificate is mandatory for all pensioners who wish to continue receiving their pension without interruption. For the year 2025, the standard submission period is betweenTo make the process more comfortable for the elderly, the government allows pensioners aged 80 years and above to begin the submission process earlier, from 1 October 2025. This extended window gives senior citizens more time and flexibility to complete the procedure without facing last-minute difficulties.The Jeevan Pramaan process involves two main steps: registering the biometric device and generating the digital certificate.Before using the service, pensioners or operators must register their biometric device such as a fingerprint or iris scanner. During registration, the user provides their Aadhaar number and mobile number, after which an OTP (One-Time Password) is sent to their mobile. After entering the OTP, they must provide their name and email ID before scanning their fingerprint or iris.Once authentication is complete, the system confirms that device registration was successful, allowing the user to move to the next step.In this step, the pensioner enters personal details such as Aadhaar number, mobile number, name, PPO number, pension type, sanctioning authority, disbursing agency, and bank account details. The system also asks whether the pensioner has remarried or is re-employed.After entering all details, the pensioner scans their fingerprint or iris using the registered biometric device. Upon successful authentication, the Digital Life Certificate is generated, and an SMS acknowledgment with the Jeevan Pramaan Certificate ID is sent to the registered mobile number.This certificate is automatically stored in the Life Certificate Repository, accessible anytime to both pensioners and pension agencies.Once the Digital Life Certificate is created, it can be electronically delivered to the concerned Pension Disbursing Agency (PDA). PDAs can log in to the Jeevan Pramaan portal to view, verify, or download the certificate directly.Pensioners can also download a PDF copy of their certificate by visiting the Jeevan Pramaan website and entering their Pramaan ID or Aadhaar number. This ensures transparency and easy access for future reference.The government provides several options to submit the Life Certificate, allowing pensioners to choose the method that suits them best.Pensioners who prefer traditional methods can visit their bank branch or a Citizen Service Centre (CSC) to submit their Life Certificate physically. Trained staff at these centres assist pensioners in verifying their details and completing the process.For those unable to travel due to age or health issues, India Post Payments Bank (IPPB) offers a doorstep banking service. Pensioners can schedule an appointment, and an IPPB representative will visit their home to complete the submission using biometric authentication. This service is available for both IPPB and non-IPPB account holders.For pensioners comfortable with technology, the Jeevan Pramaan portal provides a completely online process. By visiting https://jeevanpramaan.gov.in, pensioners can submit their Digital Life Certificate using a computer, laptop, or smartphone equipped with a fingerprint or iris scanner.The system uses the Aadhaar platform to authenticate biometric details. Once verification is successful, a Digital Life Certificate is generated and stored in a secure online repository. Pension disbursing agencies can then directly access the certificate, removing the need for physical paperwork.The UMANG (Unified Mobile Application for New-age Governance) app offers another easy option. Pensioners can download the app from the Google Play Store, search for “Jeevan Pramaan,” and select “Generate Life Certificate.”After entering the necessary details such as Aadhaar number, PPO number, and type of pension, pensioners must authenticate themselves using biometric verification. The app then generates a Digital Life Certificate (DLC), which is automatically shared with the pension disbursing authority.All pensioners who receive government, defence, or private sector pensions through authorised disbursing agencies are required to submit their Life Certificate every year. This includes:Failure to submit the certificate within the stipulated period may result in temporary suspension of pension payments until verification is completed.To ensure a smooth submission process, pensioners should keep certain details and documents ready. These include:Having these details ready helps to avoid delays or errors during authentication, whether submitting online or offline.Yes, an Aadhaar number or Virtual ID (VID) is mandatory to generate a Digital Life Certificate. The entire Jeevan Pramaan system is based on Aadhaar authentication, which ensures accuracy and prevents fraud. Pensioners must make sure their Aadhaar is linked with their mobile number and bank account before attempting to generate the certificate.The Digital Life Certificate is fully valid and legally recognised under the Information Technology Act. Pension disbursing authorities across India accept it as an official document, equivalent to the traditional paper-based life certificate.Its biometric verification system makes it secure and tamper-proof, reducing the chances of wrongful pension disbursal or misuse of pension funds.Before the introduction of Jeevan Pramaan, pensioners often faced difficulties in submitting their certificates, especially those with health problems or limited mobility. The digital system has removed these challenges, allowing pensioners to submit their verification anytime and anywhere.By integrating biometric technology and Aadhaar authentication, Jeevan Pramaan has streamlined pension management, improving efficiency for both pensioners and government departments. It also enhances transparency and ensures that genuine beneficiaries receive payments on time.
Article Summary: A Life Certificate is an official document that confirms a pensioner is alive. Without it, pension payments are automatically paused by the pension disbursing agency. This verification is essential for preventing fraudulent claims and ensuring that pensions reach the rightful beneficiaries. The Digital Life Certificate (DLC), also known as Jeevan Pramaan, is an advanced biometric- enabled version of the traditional paper certificate.

Article Title: What are gold, silver hallmarking charges? Check details and importance
Article Text:
What is gold, silver hallmarking?



Hallmarking charges



ALSO READ |

Testing for purity



The importance of hallmarking



Old hallmarking signs



ALSO READ |

Hallmark symbols



HUID in the hallmarked gold jewellery



Key components of hallmark on gold jewellery



Gold, silver hallmarking : Gold and silver jewllery have long been treasured for their beauty and value. However, ensuring their purity and authenticity is important, especially in a market prone to fraudulent practices. This is where the hallmarking scheme comes into play. Here’s what you need to know about hallmarking charges and its procedures:Hallmarking is the process of accurately determining and officially recording the proportionate content of precious metals in articles, such as gold and silver.It serves as a guarantee of their purity or fineness. In India, hallmarking is governed by stringent standards outlined in IS 15820, according to an ET report.Hallmarking charges are applied per article, regardless of its weight. For gold articles, the charge is Rs 45 per article, with a minimum charge of Rs 200 for a consignment. Similarly, for silver articles, the charge is Rs 35 per article, with a minimum charge of Rs 150 for a consignment. It's important to note that additional taxes and levies may apply.A consignment refers to a collection of articles with the same fineness, from which a sample is drawn and assayed for compliance. Different lots may exist within a consignment.Anyone can have their hallmarked jewellery tested for purity at an A&H centre by paying a testing fee of Rs. 200 (for testing using the fire assay method as per IS 1418). These centres must be recognized by the BIS. The list of recognized A&H centres is available on the BIS website under the hallmarking section.The primary objective of hallmarking is to safeguard consumers from counterfeit products. To achieve this, the Indian government made hallmarking mandatory for gold jewellery from June 16, 2021. Since April 1, 2023, jewellers are prohibited from selling any gold jewellery without a 6-digit Hallmark Unique Identification ( HUID ) number.If you bought gold jewellery on or before June 30, 2021, it may have old hallmarking signs. These signs include the purity/fineness mark and the BIS emblem, along with two additional indications: the jeweller's identification number and the assay centre's mark/number.However, starting July 1, 2021, these two indications were replaced with a unique alphanumeric HUID code. The hallmark remains valid for the lifetime of the jewellery, and consumers can sell old jewellery, whether hallmarked or un-hallmarked, to jewellers.The HUID, or hallmark Unique Identification number, is a key component of hallmarking in gold jewellery. It's a unique six-digit alphanumeric code assigned to each hallmarked item, allowing for traceability. Customers can verify the HUID number using the 'Verify HUID' feature in the BIS Care App.Since the introduction of the HUID on July 1, 2021, the hallmark on gold jewellery consists of three marks: the BIS logo, the purity of the article in caratage and fineness, and a unique six-digit alphanumeric HUID number.
Article Summary: Hallmarking is the process of accurately determining and officially recording the proportionate content of precious metals in articles, such as gold and silver. In India, hallmarking is governed by stringent standards outlined in IS 15820. Since April 1, 2023, jewellers are prohibited from selling any gold jewellery without a 6-digit Hallmark Unique Identification number.

Article Text:

Points to keep in mind

Update your will regularly: You can keep your will up-to-date by regularly updating it on the platform to reflect any changes in your life or financial situation.

Communicate with family: It's also important to store your will safely and let your family know where to find it when needed.

Creating a will online: If you are planning to create a will, many online platforms offer easy-to-use tools and resources, allowing you to draft and finalise a will without leaving your home. Here's how to make an online will.Begin by choosing a reliable online service. Look for a platform that has a simple interface, comprehensive templates, and secure storage for your documents.To register, enter your personal information like your full name, date of birth, address, and contact details. Then create a secure username and password to access the online service.Next, make a list of all your assets. Include property, investments, bank accounts, insurance policies, and any valuable items you own.Decide who will inherit your assets by choosing your beneficiaries. You can assign specific items to each beneficiary and select backup beneficiaries in case your first choices can't inherit. Then, choose an executor, the person who will carry out your will.Use one of the customizable templates available on the platform. Follow the instructions to fill in the details, specifying how you want your assets distributed and including any special conditions or terms.You can review the drafted will to ensure it's accurate and make changes if needed. If you have complicated issues, it's best to get legal advice. Once you're satisfied, you can legally execute the will according to the platform's rules, usually by electronically signing it with witnesses. The completed will is then stored securely online, with encryption for added security.With inputs from Centre for Investment Education and Learning content which appeared in Economic Times
Article Summary: If you are planning to create a will, many online platforms offer easy-to-use tools and resources, allowing you to draft and finalise a will without leaving your home. To register, enter your personal information like your full name, date of birth, address, and contact details. Then create a secure username and password to access the online service.

Article Title: How to get your CIBIL credit score without a PAN card
Article Text:
Your credit score is a three-digit number that reflects your creditworthiness and repayment capacity.

What is a CIBIL score?

How to get your CIBIL Credit score without a PAN Card

Factors affecting your CIBIL score

CIBIL credit score : Understanding your creditworthiness is pivotal in today's financial landscape, especially when seeking loans or credit cards. The Credit Information Bureau (India) Limited, commonly known as CIBIL, plays an important role in this regard by providing credit scores to borrowers.According to ET, credit score helps banks make informed decisions about extending credit cards and loans. Your credit history influences your CIBIL score , which is calculated using a special method.Your CIBIL score is a three-digit number that reflects your creditworthiness and repayment capacity. Ranging from 300 to 900, a higher score indicates a stronger likelihood of loan approval. Banks and financial institutions use this score to assess the risk associated with lending money.1. Go to the official CIBIL website and click on 'Personal CIBIL score,' then 'Get Your Free CIBIL score.' Follow the link provided and fill in all the required details.2. If you don’t have a PAN Card, you can use an alternative identification such as Passport, Voter ID, Driver’s License, or Ration Card. Enter the number of the chosen ID.3. Provide your date of birth, PIN code, and select your state. Finally, enter your mobile number and click on ‘Accept and Continue’.4. Authenticate your identity by entering the OTP (One-Time Password) delivered to your mobile device on the next screen. Click 'Continue' to proceed.5. Choose whether you want to link the device with your account. Select 'Yes' or 'No' based on your preference.6. Upon successful registration, you will see a page confirming that 'You have successfully registered!'7. Click on ‘Go to Dashboard’ to access and view your CIBIL score.The factors influencing the new CIBIL score, as stated on the CIBIL website, include:1. Depth of credit: This refers to the duration of your existing credit history, calculated from the opening date of your oldest credit account.2. Long-term trend of outstanding balances: The overall trend of outstanding balances over time affects your score.3. Transaction history on credit cards: How you manage transactions on your credit cards impacts your score.4. Ratio of actual repayment amount to total amount due: This ratio indicates your repayment behavior and influences your score accordingly.5. Number of new accounts opened and number of accounts closed: Opening or closing accounts can affect your score, so managing these actions wisely is important.
Article Summary: Your CIBIL score is a three-digit number that reflects your creditworthiness and repayment capacity. Ranging from 300 to 900, a higher score indicates a stronger likelihood of loan approval. Banks and financial institutions use this score to assess the risk associated with lending money.

Article Title: Airlines turn to AI to save fuel; IndiGo to start trials for thriftier take-offs from today
Article Text:
Airlines turn to AI to save fuel; IndiGo to start trials for thriftier take-offs

NEW DELHI: After doing everything humanely possible to cut fuel burn, Indian airlines have turned to artificial intelligence (AI) to help save the prohibitively expensive black gold. IndiGo has asked its pilots to save fuel during the time when the aircraft is most thirsty - climb which is done full throttle (car equivalent of the gas pedal being floored) - by opting for “OptiClimb solution” starting Thursday on trial basis on its Airbus fleet.In simple terms, instead of taking off at a constant speed, they will do so at varying speeds wherever possible. The airline aims to save 60-65 kilo per take off using this AI solution. Multiplying that with its 2,000-odd flights, the saving could be impressive even if half of them use this. Lower fuel burn means lower emissions too. Depending on the outcome of the trial period flights, the airline will decide on making it a regular feature on its Airbus fleet.“Over the past several months, we have been working closely with SITA (world's leading flight operation solution provider) on the implementation of their OptiClimb solution (which is) an AI-powered flight optimisation solution that helps pilots reduce fuel burn and carbon emissions during the energy-intensive climb phase of a flight,” a mail sent by Captain Ashim Mittra, IndiGo’s senior VP (flight operations), to its about 6,000 pilots on Wednesday night says.“The climb phase typically accounts for the highest rate of fuel consumption during any flight…. Recent advancements in aircraft performance analytics have enabled more refined climb optimisation techniques…. By considering actual aircraft performance, planned take-off weight, cruise altitude, and prevailing atmospheric conditions, OptiClimb enables more efficient climb profiles, delivering measurable reductions in fuel consumption and associated carbon emissions while maintaining the highest standards of operational safety and regulatory compliance,” the mail adds.In contemporary times, fuel saving has become a necessity since the 2008 subprime crisis when global economy was slowing down amid energy prices shooting up. At that time, airlines made aircraft lighter by stripping away non essential onboard things like curtains, partitions, carrying less water. More fuel efficient engines were developed over time. The next gen of fuel efficient aircraft using completely different engine architecture is in the works but is at least a decade away if not more. Till they become a reality, AI could help airlines save some fuel.“The primary objective of this (OptiClimb) initiative is to enhance fuel efficiency, reduce our carbon footprint, and strengthen operational sustainability, while ensuring full adherence to Air Traffic Control (ATC) requirements and safe operating practices. As airlines worldwide continue to focus on operational excellence and environmental stewardship, the adoption of data-driven solutions such as OptiClimb demonstrates how advanced analytics can unlock incremental efficiencies beyond those achievable through conventional Flight Management System (FMS) optimisation alone,” Captain Mittra’s email adds.
Article Summary: IndiGo has asked its pilots to save fuel during the time when the aircraft is most thirsty - climb. The airline aims to save 60-65 kilo per take off using this AI solution. Depending on the outcome of the trial period flights, the airline will decide on making it a regular feature on its Airbus fleet.

Article Title: Summer travel hack: How to use your credit card to save big
Article Text:
Aligning travelling preferences and plans with the right credit card ensures that the benefits align with your actual spending. (AI image)

Evaluate Core Benefits on a Travel Credit Card

Reward Earning Rate:

Credit card for travel: 4 things to check

Redemption Value:

Transfer Partners:

Terms and Conditions:

Understand Your Travel Preferences Before Choosing a Card

One fee can increase travel cost

Look Beyond Rewards, Consider Travel Perks that Matter

Save on International Travel with Zero Forex Mark-up Cards



Use Wisely, Maximize Value from Your Travel Credit Card

Understand various layers

(The author, Santosh Agarwal is the CEO of Paisabazaar)

Summer is peak travel season with high demand leading to higher costs, and travellers actively looking for smart ways to save on flight and hotel bookings. While travel deals, seasonal discounts and booking offers can help reduce costs to an extent, the right credit cards can go a long way in maximizing savings on travel spends. From free flight tickets and hotel stays to lounge access and accelerated rewards on travel bookings, smart planning and the right credit card can significantly reduce overall travel expenses when used smartly.When selecting a travel credit card, you should focus on the overall value proposition instead of the headline benefits. Ideally, core benefits on travel-centric cards should be evaluated across four major parameters - reward earning rate, redemption value, transfer partners and associated terms and conditions.The key thing to know is how many points or miles you will earn through your spending across different categories. While most credit cards offer a decent base reward rate, the real value often comes from accelerated earning on travel and other bonus categories.Airline or portal co-branded cards typically offer higher earnings on their associated platforms. Many premium and super-premium cards provide significantly higher returns on travel bookings made through their own reward marketplaces.Moreover, several cards offer bonus rewards or travel vouchers on reaching spending milestones which can again help high-spending travellers maximize returns from their cards.Hence, travellers should carefully evaluate where and how they intend to use the card to maximize earnings.Some cards may offer higher reward earning rate leading to quicker point accumulation but if the redemption value is low, effective returns will not be good. Hence, checking the redemption value is equally important. Travellers must assess the value that each point delivers when redeeming them against flights, hotels or other travel benefits.The utility of a travel card for you depends on its list of transfer partners. A card may offer excellent value-back on travel but if the partner airlines or hotels do not align with your travel preferences, the benefits may remain underutilized. Hence, always check the transfer partners and the point-to-mile conversion ratio for your preferred partner before selecting a card.While the above three core parameters are the most important, each may come with associated terms and conditions, directly affecting the actual savings. Most cards cap total reward earnings in the accelerated categories, which could be a key factor for high-spenders looking to maximize value. Some cards also put restrictions on the points that you can redeem. Similarly, some cards may have slightly complex redemption structures.A credit card that works well for a frequent international traveller may not necessarily be the best fit for someone who takes domestic vacations. So, instead of applying for the most popular travel card in the market, you should first evaluate your travel preferences and choose a card that can provide the highest value.For instance, travellers who prefer flying with a particular carrier may benefit more from airline co-branded credit cards. These cards generally offer accelerated earning in the form of the airline’s loyalty currency or air miles, along with benefits such as priority boarding, discounted tickets or complimentary flight vouchers. On the other hand, those who want flexibility should consider premium travel cards that allow reward point transfers across multiple airline and hotel partners.Aligning travelling preferences and plans with the right credit card ensures that the benefits align with your actual spending.While reward earning, redemption value and transfer partners determine the core value proposition of a travel card, additional travel perks can further enhance the overall experience and savings for frequent travellers.Complimentary domestic and international airport lounge access remains one of the most popular benefits sought by frequent flyers. Most cards offer a limited number of visits per quarter for primary users, whereas premium and super-premium cards may provide unlimited access, often extended to add-on card users as well.Some premium travel-centric cards also come with complimentary hotel memberships, room upgrades, airport meet and greet and priority concierge services, making the overall travel experience much more convenient for you.While these additional perks do deliver meaningful value, you must assess whether they will genuinely utilize these benefits. For instance, getting a high-fee super-premium card only for a complimentary meet and greet once a year may not be the smartest idea. Instead, you should first focus on the core benefits and choose a card that provides the right balance of value-back, meaningful perks and manageable annual costs.Most credit cards charge up to 3.5% forex mark-up fee when you make a transaction in a foreign currency. This increases the overall cost of foreign travel and can quietly become a major expense for frequent international travellers.Several travel-focused cards come with low or zero forex mark-up benefits. Those who are planning overseas vacations this summer should also consider forex mark-up charges carefully in addition to rewards and additional travel perks.Simply owning a travel credit card is not enough. The real savings come from using the card strategically. Since most travel cards offer accelerated value-back on travel expenses, cardholders should try to route major travel expenses such as flight tickets, hotel bookings and holiday packages through the right card. Card issuers also run limited period offers across online travel portals, airlines or hotels which can further increase savings.When it comes to redemption, you should check all available options and their point-to-value ratio and then redeem wisely. Most cards would provide better value if you choose point-to-mile conversion with partner airlines or hotels than direct cashback or vouchers. Additionally, using lounge access, complimentary memberships and travel privileges regularly can substantially improve the effective value derived from the card.While most travel cards are designed to reward high-spenders handsomely, you should keep an eye on their expenditure and spend only as much as they can afford to pay back on time. Overspending merely to achieve spending milestones or unlock additional perks can potentially lead to debt.As summer travel continues to rise, choosing the right travel credit card can make a significant difference to the overall travel budget. However, the key is not just selecting a popular card but in understanding how the rewards work, where the real value lies and which benefits genuinely align with your travel habits.
Article Summary: Summer is peak travel season with high demand leading to higher costs. Travellers are looking for smart ways to save on flight and hotel bookings. The right credit cards can go a long way in maximizing savings on travel spends. From free flight tickets and hotel stays to lounge access and accelerated rewards on travel bookings, smart planning can significantly reduce overall travel expenses.

Article Title: Star Health deploys AI advisor to aid agents in proposals, claims
Article Text:
File photo

.

Mumbai: Star Health has started using its AI-enabled application ATOM PRO as a digital advisor for agents, aiming to improve policy explanation, speed up issuance, and streamline claims processing. The company is targeting 50% of its claims to be settled using AI in two years.According to the company, the new platform shifts focus from backend complexity to customer-facing clarity, even though it operates on the agent’s device. The application acts as a real-time support tool during policy sales. It lays out product features, premium details, riders, and waiting periods on screen, reducing ambiguity at the point of sale. This addresses a persistent concern among buyers about hidden clauses such as sub-limits or exclusions that typically surface only at the time of claims. By clarifying coverage upfront, the platform aims to reduce disputes arising from misinterpretation.“For customers, buying health insurance should not feel complex or time-consuming. They should be able to understand what a plan offers, compare relevant options, know the waiting periods and see accurate pricing before making a decision. ATOM PRO is helping our agents bring that clarity and speed into the purchase journey, while enabling to serve customers with greater confidence,” said Anand Roy, MD & CEO, Star HealthThe rollout has also compressed policy issuance timelines. According to the company, more than 95% of retail policies are now processed digitally, and 80% are issued within five minutes. This replaces a system that earlier relied on paper forms, manual checks, and multi-day underwriting cycles. Buyers can now complete selection, payment, and receive policy documents in a single sitting.The platform’s backend integration extends to claims. Digital reimbursement modules have increased the share of digital claim submissions from 8% to 21%. Agents can upload documents directly, bypassing physical submission processes and reducing delays in claim registration and processing.
Article Summary: Star Health has started using its AI-enabled application ATOM PRO as a digital advisor for agents. It aims to improve policy explanation, speed up issuance, and streamline claims processing. The company is targeting 50% of its claims to be settled using AI in two years.

Thinking about dental implants abroad in 2026? Turkey almost certainly belongs on your shortlist. The same premium implant systems used across Western Europe are available here at 60–70% lower prices, fitted by internationally trained specialists in clinics built around international patients. This guide explains why Turkey leads the market, how pricing works, and how to tell serious operators from informal ones, before ranking the ten clinics setting the standard this year.Vera Smile is widely regarded as one of top dental implant clinics in Turkey , backed by their American Academy of Cosmetic Dentistry (AACD) recognition and Slow Dentistry® membership. As a sub-brand of Vera Clinic (one of Turkey's most internationally recognised medical groups), it benefits from the group's R&D infrastructure, including Vera Academy, which develops next-generation technologies such as Vector-10™.Treatments use premium systems (Straumann and Nobel Biocare) supported by Digital Smile Design and CAD/CAM workflows, covering the full spectrum from single and multiple implants to All-on-4 and All-on-6 full-arch solutions, with transparent pricing: implants from €450, crowns from €200, veneers from €275. Vera Smile uses Hiossen IMP, NDI Medical, Straumann, J Dental Care, and Neodent implants, and is in high demand among international patients, so booking well in advance is the safest way to secure your preferred treatment window.Kristal Clinic offers 3D-guided placement, CAD/CAM prosthetic design, and digital smile mock-ups before any procedure begins. They also offer multilingual coordinators, VIP transfers, and full travel coordination support for international patients. Implants range from €470, crowns from €210, and veneers from €280. They use Straumann, Nobel Biocare, Hiossen, Medigma, IRES, BEGO, Venus, and DTI. Requesting the full 3D plan before booking flights is worthwhile, since pre-arrival planning is a key strength.A specialist team reports 96.5% success for full-arch cases, using Straumann and Nobel Biocare with 3D CBCT imaging, CAD/CAM, and laser dentistry. It includes a lifetime implant guarantee, 5-year crown warranty, and same-day protocols for selected full-arch cases, plus support in eight languages. They use Straumann, Nobel Biocare, and Medigma. The same-day protocol can compress treatment into a single visit.WestDent Clinic reports a 95%+ success rate with detailed digital planning. Eight treatment rooms with extended hours, a full implant portfolio plus orthodontics and gum surgery, and CAD/CAM milling. Implants from €500, crowns from €210, veneers from €290. They use Straumann (Official Provider), Nobel Biocare, MODE, NTA, Bicon, Osstem, and Medentika. Its pre-treatment mapping is among the most detailed in the region.The clinic is known for advanced surgical work: All-on-4/6, sinus lifts, and bone grafting, with European-standard sterilisation. It offers zirconia crowns, veneers, and long-term warranties, including lifetime guarantees on select cases. Implants from €450, crowns from €200, All-on-4 from €3,500. Dentavrasya uses FDA-approved systems, though brands aren't publicly named. For bone loss or full-mouth cases, asking about the grafting and All-on-4 history is where its expertise shows most.Founded in 2013, the clinic is Health Tourism certified by the Turkish Ministry of Health. It specialises in zirconia crowns, veneers, Hollywood Smile, and implant restorations, with personalised digital plans prepared before travel. Implants from €480, crowns from €200, veneers from €275. They use titanium-based implants (brands not named). Sharing x-rays in advance lets Dentevim build a full plan before you arrive.Dentatur is a dual-location clinic offering all-inclusive packages covering treatment, hotel, and VIP transfers, with in-house labs enabling same-week restorations. They have a strong UK and German patient base. Implants from €450, crowns from €180, veneers from €250. Dentatur uses Straumann, Nobel Biocare, and Astra Tech. Confirming inclusions before booking matters, since accommodation and transfers vary between branches.Established in 2008 on the Aegean coast, Marmaris Dental Center is popular with UK and Scandinavian patients. Oral surgeons and prosthodontists here handle implants and restorative work using CE-certified materials, with plans built around short trips. Implant prices start from €500, crowns from €220, and veneers from €280. Uses Nobel Biocare, Straumann, and Alpha Bio. The coastal setting suits the rest period between placement and final restoration.Alanya Dental Clinic is one of the Riviera's longest-standing clinics, founded in 1993, with ISO-certified sterilisation and three decades of international experience. Its on-site lab enables 5–7 day timelines. Implant prices start from €480, crowns from €200, and veneers from €270. Alanya Dental Clinic uses a mix of foreign premium and Turkish brands (not disclosed). The quick turnaround pairs easily with a holiday in Alanya.EsteDent Turkey is reputed for smile-led work, including implant-supported Hollywood Smile cases. It specialises in E-max veneers, zirconia crowns, gum contouring, and whitening, using minimally invasive techniques and advanced imaging. It’s popular with younger patients. Veneer prices start from €270, crowns from €200, and whitening from €180. Its implant brand has not been disclosed. Its before-and-after galleries give the clearest read on its aesthetic style.Turkey hosts over a million international patients annually, with dentistry among its most in-demand specialities. Internationally trained specialists, direct flights, and state-backed infrastructure coordinated by USHAS make it a natural hub. Cities like Istanbul, Izmir, Antalya, and Alanya host hundreds of clinics offering multilingual coordinators, hotel partnerships, and treatments streamlined for one- or two-week trips for dental implant treatments in Turkey The significant price difference for the same premium implant system is due to Turkey's lower operational overhead, government support (tax incentives and VAT exemptions), and favourable exchange rates, which keep margins lean compared to Western countries.The selection process relies on five checks: verifying accreditations (MoH license, ISO 9001, AACD/ITI), material transparency (named implant/crown brands), clear, itemised pricing, verifiable dentist credentials, and positive independent reviews. A clinic scoring well on all five is the safest option.
Article Summary: Vera Smile is widely regarded as one of top dental implant clinics in Turkey. The same premium implant systems used across Western Europe are available here at 60–70% lower prices. This guide explains why Turkey leads the market, how pricing works, and how to tell serious operators from informal ones.

Article Title: China beats Elon Musk's 'Jesus-level technology' to launch world's first commercial brain chip
Article Text:
The 'Jesus-level technology'



How China's NEO differs from Neuralink



China has moved ahead of Elon Musk 's 'Jesus-level technology' Neuralink in the race to commercialise brain-computer interface technology, approving what is being described as the world's first commercially available brain chip after successful clinical trials.The coin-sized implant, called NEO, has become the first surgically implanted brain-computer interface (BCI) device to clear clinical testing for commercial use, according to the New York Times report.Developed by researchers at Beijing's Tsinghua University and Shanghai-based Neuracle Technology, the device is expected to enter mass production for China's state-run healthcare system.The first version of NEO is aimed at helping patients with spinal cord injuries and paralysis regain control over parts of their nervous system.But researchers, technology companies and investors see brain-computer interfaces as the beginning of a much larger shift in how humans interact with machines.The development comes as Musk's Neuralink continues human trials in the United States but is yet to receive broad regulatory approval for commercial use.Brain-computer interfaces allow signals generated by the brain to be converted into digital commands that can control computers, devices or prosthetics.Musk has repeatedly promoted the technology's potential to transform lives.Speaking via video link at an event in Israel this week, Musk argued that his company, Neuralink, does not receive enough recognition for its achievements."Restoring control of people who are tetraplegics and restoring sight, I think, are pretty big deals," Musk said. "They're sort of what I might call Jesus-level technologies," he added.Neuralink has said its implants could eventually allow users to type, move a computer cursor and operate devices using only their thoughts.Supporters of the technology envision an even more ambitious future, where people could communicate digitally through thoughts or interact with machines without physical input.One reason analysts believe China has moved ahead more quickly is the design of the NEO implant. Unlike Neuralink's N1 device, which requires electrodes to penetrate the brain's cerebral cortex, NEO is designed to sit between the skull and the brain.Eight sensors are placed against the dura mater, the brain's protective outer membrane reducing the invasiveness of the procedure. The implant captures brain signals and sends them to nearby computers, where they are translated into digital commands.According to reports, 36 patients have already tested the device with positive results. Researchers believe the less-invasive approach may help reduce some of the medical risks traditionally associated with brain implants, the Post reported.Experts say brain-computer interfaces could improve the lives of millions of people suffering from neurological disorders, particularly those affecting movement and speech. Potential applications extend beyond paralysis and spinal injuries to conditions such as Parkinson's disease, epilepsy, stroke and depression.However, the technology has also raised concerns about privacy, security and safety. Griffith University cybersecurity expert Dr David Tuffley said the devices offer enormous potential but also create new risks."Brain implants may sound dystopian, but they are a promising part of neuroscience research," he said. At the same time, he warned that the devices could "theoretically allow hackers to access sensitive neural data, such as patients' thoughts and memories.""Hacking may also enable them to impair a patient's cognitive functions such as the ability to concentrate, or even manipulate motor signals to affect how well they move," he said."That's a scary prospect, especially if these devices become more common," he added. Despite rapid progress, researchers also caution that brain implants still face significant biological hurdles.Neuralink's N1 implant is currently being tested in nine patients. One participant, Audrey Crews, recently described the impact the technology has already had on her life."I tried writing my name for the first time in 20 years. I'm working on it," she wrote on X. "It's humbling to know my journey is helping Neuralink refine this technology, which could one day let millions control devices with their minds," she added.
Article Summary: China has approved what is being described as the world's first commercially available brain chip. The coin-sized implant, called NEO, has become the first surgically implanted brain-computer interface (BCI) device to clear clinical testing for commercial use. NEO is expected to enter mass production for China's state-run healthcare system.

Article Title: Quote of the day by Larry Page: “If you're changing the world, you're working on important things”
Article Text:
Larry Page

Quote of the day by Google co-founder Larry Page



If you're

changing the world

, you're working on important things. You're excited to get up in the morning.

What Larry Page is really saying



Why excitement is worth taking as a signal



About Google co-founder Larry Page



There is a moment many successful people quietly dread. It is not failure, but the moment when there is nothing left to chase. The salary target has been met with the promotion and the business is stable. From the outside, it looks like the finish line. But for some people this is not the time to relax as that moment feels nothing like relief. If anything, the work becomes more consuming, not less. New problems start looking interesting and the old goals start feeling oddly small. The question stops being “how do I get there?” and starts being “what else is worth doing?” Larry Page, the man who co-founded Google out of a dorm room and went on to reshape how the entire world finds information, understood that feeling better than most. And his quote captures something that most conventional thinking about ambition and success quietly gets wrong.On the surface, the quote reads like a motivational line you might find on a coffee mug. But sit with it for a moment, and it starts to feel more like a diagnostic question than a pep talk. Page is not saying that important work is always exciting, he is saying something more specific: that excitement that makes you want to get out of bed in the morning is a signal that tells you whether what you are doing actually matters to you, or whether you have just convinced yourself that it does.Most people have experienced both versions of a working day. There are days when a task feels connected to something real, which is a problem worth solving, a person worth helping, an idea worth testing. And there are days when the same number of hours pass in a fog of routine, where nothing feels connected to anything in particular. Page is pointing at the excitement he describes is not enthusiasm for its own sake. It is what happens when the problem in front of you is genuinely bigger than you are — when you do not yet know how to solve it, and that uncertainty feels like opportunity rather than threat.Most career advice focuses on outcomes, which can be measured in salaries, titles, milestones and recognition. These tend to dominate the conversation and social media reinforces this by turning professional achievements into public moments such as funding rounds, promotions and acquisitions which become signals that something meaningful has happened.Page’s quote quietly reframes the question. Instead of asking what you have achieved, it asks how you feel when the alarm goes off in the morning. That might sound soft or imprecise next to a balance sheet, but it turns out to be one of the more honest ways to measure whether work is aligned with what you actually care about.Larry Page built Google into one of the most powerful companies in human history. By any conventional measure, he could have stopped at dozens of different points along the way. He did not, and the quote explains that it wa not that stopping was impossible, but because the problem was still interesting. And the world got Google.Larry Page grew up in Michigan, the son of two computer scientists, in a house filled with technology magazines and a quiet assumption that problems were things to be solved rather than avoided.He studied computer engineering before arriving at Stanford, where he became interested in the mathematical structure of the internet.His early research into how web pages linked to each other and the idea eventually became the foundation of the PageRank algorithm, the engine behind Google's original search system. What began as a research project became, within a few years, one of the most used tools in human history.Page served as Google's CEO during its formative years, stepped back, and then returned to the role in 2011. In 2015, he became CEO of Alphabet, the parent company he helped design to give Google's various long-term projects the space to operate independently.
Article Summary: Google co-founder Larry Page's quote captures something that most conventional thinking about ambition and success quietly gets wrong. Page is not saying that important work is always exciting, he is saying that excitement that makes you want to get out of bed in the morning is a signal that tells you whether what you are doing actually matters.

Article Title: Scientists in America are using pigeons to train medical AI tools for early stage cancer detection
Article Text:
How pigeons became part of the AI medical tool research



“look totally different from a lung nodule,”

“Right now, I’m constraining myself purely to medical misses because those for me seem far more practical. But I am hoping to do a little bit of ‘can we tell which Caravaggio are real and which ones are fake?’”

Artificial intelligence (AI) researchers developing medical AI tools have a new inspiration: pigeons. Researchers in the US are reportedly studying pigeons' visual abilities to help develop AI tools that could improve the early detection of cancer and reduce the number of abnormalities missed during medical scans. The research is being led by Dr Gregory DiGirolamo of the College of the Holy Cross in Worcester, Massachusetts. His work focuses on understanding how radiologists process medical images and how AI can be trained to identify subtle signs of disease that may go unnoticed during routine examinations.Researchers and radiologists can miss abnormalities in some scans despite looking directly at them. Earlier research by DiGirolamo and his colleagues found that when radiologists viewed suspicious lung nodules on CT scans, their eyes often lingered on the area and their pupils widened, even when they later classified the scan as normal. According to a report by Popular Science, the findings suggest that the brain may detect abnormalities at a non-conscious level before that information reaches conscious decision-making.To better understand this non-conscious visual system, DiGirolamo and his team turned to pigeons. The researchers trained six pigeons to watch short CT scan videos and determine whether they contained lung nodules, growths that can sometimes indicate lung cancer.During the training process, some pigeons received food rewards for correctly identifying scans with nodules, while others were rewarded for correctly recognising normal scans. Over time, the birds learned to distinguish between the two and were able to apply that knowledge to scans they had not previously seen.The study also found that pigeons could recognise other lung abnormalities, including emphysema and ground-glass nodules, despite not being specifically trained to identify them. Ground-glass nodules can be associated with early-stage lung cancer.To the human eye, emphysema and ground-glass nodulesDiGirolamo told Popular Science. However, the pigeons' performance suggested that the conditions may share a visual pattern detectable by the visual system.DiGirolamo hopes to use these insights to build AI systems that can assist doctors in identifying abnormalities that might otherwise be overlooked.In this case, the suggested process involves obtaining eye-tracking and physiological data from radiologists as they analyse medical images. This information is further used by AI systems to detect minute signs of anomalies, even if the radiologist concludes that the images are normal.DiGirolamo said the technology is intended to support rather than replace medical professionals. The goal is to create AI tools that learn from radiologists' visual responses and help bridge the gap between conscious and non-conscious perception during image analysis.The researcher believes similar methods could eventually be applied in other fields, including cardiology, art authentication and security screening. However, he noted that his current focus remains on healthcare applications.DiGirolamo said.
Article Summary: Researchers in the US are reportedly studying pigeons' visual abilities to help develop AI tools. The research is being led by Dr Gregory DiGirolamo of the College of the Holy Cross in Worcester, Massachusetts. His work focuses on understanding how radiologists process medical images and how AI can be trained to identify subtle signs of disease.

Article Title: In 1957, the Wham-O founders, watching a pie tin sail across a lawn, unexpectedly helped create the Frisbee we still use today
Article Text:
A Frisbee made by Wham-O. Image Credits: Wikimedia Commons

A campus tradition before the Frisbee

From a pie tin to a plastic disc

Walter Frederick Morrison promoting his Pluto Platters. Image Credits: Wikimedia Commons

Why 1957 became a turning point

Why the Frisbee matters

Innovation does not always begin with a breakthrough. Sometimes it begins with people having fun. In the case of the Frisbee, a pastime that started with students tossing pie tins, eventually caught the attention of entrepreneurs who saw more than a flying object. They saw a product. By 1957, that vision had helped turn a simple lawn game into a toy that would become a global phenomenon.The Frisbee's story is unusual because no single person can claim to have invented it outright. Its origins stretch from university campuses, where students discovered the surprising flight of discarded pie tins, to inventors who experimented with plastic discs, and finally to the Wham-O company, which refined and popularised the idea.The roots of the Frisbee stretch back decades before Wham-O entered the picture. According to Yale University's historical records, students were tossing empty pie tins from the Frisbie Pie Company as early as the 1920s. As the tins sailed through the air, students reportedly shouted "Frisbee!" to warn others to look out.This became a routine on the university campus. Although the pie plates were not invented as toys, their shape was ideal for playing with them in this manner.Slowly, the process of throwing the flying discs became an integral part of student culture, laying the foundation for a commercially successful product.What is important about this particular history of the Frisbee is that it proves the Frisbee was not a result of an accidental invention. The basic idea had already been explored, but the challenge was to create something lighter and safer.The next significant contribution was made by inventor Fred Morrison, who started working on plastic flying discs in the 1940s. Unlike metal pie plates, these devices were stronger, more easily thrown, and better at flying.According to Illumin Magazine the chain runs from the Frisbiepie company to Yale students, then to Fred Morrison’s plastic flying disc. Morrison eventually invented the "Pluto Platter," which was a plastic flying disc that was partially designed based on the craze about flying saucers that existed among Americans at that time. In 1955, Morrison sold the invention rights to Wham-O, a California-based toy company that was known for developing unusual ideas into toys.For Wham-O, it meant the development of an already existing idea and not the creation of something new. Wham-O realised that people loved throwing discs, but the difficulty lay in developing a toy out of it.In 1957, Wham-O was manufacturing and distributing flying discs on a broader scale than ever before. They named their new product "Frisbee" partly because this was intended to differentiate the product from the Frisbie Pie Company.What made this moment significant was not the discovery that discs could fly. People had known that for decades. Instead, the secret lay in recognising that a simple recreational activity could become a successful consumer product. Wham-O standardised the design, created a memorable brand, and introduced the flying disc to a much wider audience.The company effectively transformed a traditional game into a commercial toy. A pastime that had existed on university lawns was suddenly available in toy shops across America.The Frisbee endures because of its simple design: it needs no batteries, screens, or complicated instructions. Its history can be considered an example of how innovation occurs. The Frisbee was created by a long series of developments; it was thrown around campus lawns by students using pie tins, and after several inventors tried out different materials, entrepreneurs managed to identify the market value of an existing product.In the end, what we have today is an item which has changed little since then. More than sixty years after Wham-O popularised the flying disc, the Frisbee still has the same charm it had when people watched a spinning disc sail across a lawn.
Article Summary: The Frisbee's story is unusual because no single person can claim to have invented it outright. Its origins stretch from university campuses, to inventors who experimented with plastic discs, and finally to the Wham-O company, which refined and popularised the idea. The company effectively transformed a traditional game into a commercial toy.
